name: Next.js: App Router & Server Components
description: Leverage Next.js App Router and React Server Components for optimal performance and DX.
template:
Next.js App Router Best Practices:

1. **App Router Structure**
   - Use /app directory (not /pages)
   - Route structure: app/[feature]/page.tsx
   - Use app/layout.tsx for shared layouts
   - Use app/(group) for grouped routes without URL segments

2. **React Server Components (RSC)**
   - Default: all components are server components
   - Use 'use client' directive only when needed
   - Server components: data fetching, secrets, database access
   - Client components: event listeners, hooks, useState, useEffect

3. **Server Actions & Forms**
   - Use Server Actions for mutations (posts, updates, deletes)
   - Secure: API keys, secrets stay server-side
   - Progressive enhancement: forms work without JS
   - Pattern: 'use server' directive at top of action function

4. **Data Fetching Strategies**
   - Fetch in Server Components; pass data to Client Components
   - Use revalidateTag() and revalidatePath() for ISR
   - Cache: fetch with { cache: 'force-cache' } for static data
   - Dynamic: { revalidate: 3600 } for hourly updates

5. **Streaming & Suspense**
   - Use Suspense boundaries for slow components
   - Streaming Server Components for progressive rendering
   - Pattern: <Suspense fallback={<Skeleton />}><SlowComponent /></Suspense>
   - Improves perceived performance and FCP/LCP metrics

name: Next.js: Image Optimization & Responsive Images
description: Use Next.js Image component and modern formats for optimal Core Web Vitals.
template: Next.js Image Optimization: 1. **next/image Component** - Always use component (not ) - Set explicit width and height (prevents CLS) - Use placeholder='blur' for visual feedback - Enable automatic WebP/AVIF conversion 2. **Image Formats & Responsive** - Use AVIF first, WebP fallback, JPEG base - Implement srcSet for responsive images - Use sizes attribute: sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 50vw" - Never load images 2x rendered size 3. **Priority & Loading** - Set priority={true} for above-the-fold images - Use loading='lazy' for below-the-fold images - Avoid loading='eager' on multiple images - Prefetch hero images in link tags 4. **Image Optimization Config** - Set next.config.js deviceSizes and imageSizes - Use remote pattern whitelisting for external domains - Configure quality (75-85 is good default) - Enable unoptimized={false} for CDN delivery
source: GitHub: next.js image docs, web vitals

name: Next.js: Middleware & Edge Runtime
description: Use Edge Middleware for authentication, redirects, and request transformation.
template:
Next.js Middleware Best Practices:

1. **Edge Middleware**
   - File: middleware.ts at project root (app or pages level)
   - Runs before routes are matched
   - Use for auth, redirects, headers, request modification
   - Fast execution; limited runtime (no Node.js APIs)

2. **Authentication Middleware**
   - Check auth tokens/cookies before routing
   - Redirect unauthenticated users to /login
   - Pass user info to downstream requests
   - Pattern: export function middleware(request: NextRequest)

3. **Response Headers & Security**
   - Add security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options)
   - Set cache headers for optimal CDN caching
   - Add CORS headers for API routes
   - Reference: https://securityheaders.com

4. **Request Transformation**
   - Modify headers, cookies, query params
   - Add request tracking IDs
   - Redirect based on geolocation or device type
   - Pattern: NextResponse.next() with modified headers

name: Next.js: Bundle Analysis & Code Splitting
description: Analyze and optimize JavaScript bundle size for faster loading and better performance metrics.
template:
Next.js Bundle Optimization:

1. **Bundle Analysis**
   - Use @next/bundle-analyzer package
   - Analyze production bundles: npm run build && npm run analyze
   - Identify large dependencies (lodash, moment, etc.)
   - Set bundle size budget: 200KB for landing, 300KB for app

2. **Code Splitting Strategy**
   - Route-based splitting (automatic with App Router)
   - Component-based splitting: React.lazy for modals, charts
   - Dynamic imports: import dynamically when needed
   - Pattern: const Modal = dynamic(() => import('./Modal'))

3. **Dependency Optimization**
   - Replace heavy libraries: moment → date-fns, lodash → lodash-es
   - Use tree-shaking: es2015 modules, named exports
   - Import only needed functions: import { debounce } from 'lodash-es'
   - Avoid polyfills for modern browsers

4. **Third-Party Script Optimization**
   - Use next/script with strategy prop
   - strategy='afterInteractive' for analytics, ads
   - strategy='lazyOnload' for non-critical scripts
   - Load Google Fonts with display=swap parameter

name: Performance: Google Core Web Vitals & Lighthouse
description: Achieve 100% Lighthouse score by optimizing Core Web Vitals (CLS, LCP, INP, FCP).
template:
Core Web Vitals & Lighthouse Targets:

1. **Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) < 0.1**
   - Anti-pattern: Dynamic content without dimensions
   - Fix: Always set width/height on images and media
   - Fix: Reserve space for ads, embeds before loading
   - Tool: Chrome DevTools > Performance > Layout Shifts

2. **Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) < 2.5s**
   - Optimize: Hero image (AVIF/WebP, proper sizes)
   - Optimize: Critical CSS inlining in head
   - Optimize: Lazy load below-the-fold content
   - Tool: Web Vitals library, Lighthouse

3. **Interaction to Next Paint (INP) < 200ms**
   - Fix: Offload heavy JS work to requestIdleCallback
   - Fix: Break up long tasks (>50ms) with yields
   - Fix: Debounce/throttle input handlers
   - Tool: React Profiler, Web Vitals INP metric

4. **First Contentful Paint (FCP) < 1.5s**
   - Optimize: Remove render-blocking resources
   - Optimize: Inline critical path CSS
   - Optimize: Preload critical fonts with <link rel=preload>
   - Optimization: Use CDN for static assets

5. **Time to Interactive (TTI) / Total Blocking Time (TBT) < 200ms**
   - Fix: Code split JavaScript
   - Fix: Remove unused JavaScript
   - Fix: Minify and compress assets (gzip, brotli)
   - Tool: Coverage tab in DevTools, lighthouse

6. **Lighthouse 100% Checklist**
   - Performance: All CWV targets met
   - Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA minimum
   - Best Practices: HTTPS, no console errors, no deprecated APIs
   - SEO: Meta tags, structured data, mobile-friendly

name: Performance: Memory Leak Prevention
description: Prevent memory leaks through proper cleanup, event listener management, and object lifecycle.
template:
Memory Leak Prevention:

1. **useEffect Cleanup**
   - Anti-pattern: Missing cleanup function
   - Fix: Always return cleanup function from useEffect
   - Pattern: useEffect(() => { const handler = () => {}; window.addEventListener('resize', handler); return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', handler); }, [])

2. **Event Listener Management**
   - Use AbortController for fetch and addEventListener
   - Pattern: const abort = new AbortController(); fetch(url, { signal: abort.signal })
   - Cleanup: abort.abort() in component unmount
   - Never use arrow functions in addEventListener (creates new instance each time)

3. **Closure & Reference Retention**
   - Avoid retaining large objects in closures
   - Clear references: setInterval/setTimeout → clearInterval/clearTimeout
   - Detach DOM elements from closures
   - Monitor with Chrome DevTools Heap Snapshots

4. **React Context & Memoization**
   - Memoize context values: const value = useMemo(() => ({...}), [deps])
   - Split contexts to avoid unnecessary re-renders
   - Use weak references for caches (WeakMap, WeakSet)

5. **Third-Party Library Cleanup**
   - Properly unmount third-party widgets (maps, charts, editors)
   - Call destroy() or cleanup() methods in useEffect cleanup
   - Monitor bundle size: uninstall unused libraries
   - Review dependencies quarterly for leaks and outdated packages
source: GitHub: react docs, chrome devtools memory profiling
